description: Hard rule that fires before any skill, routine, app, or standing instruction is built. Runneth asks 2-4 clarifying questions in chat, writes a plan file, delivers it as a link widget, and waits for approval before touching anything.

changelog.notes: Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.

Clarify the read-vs-write boundary. Replace 'Do not read files to begin execution' (was being misread as 'do not read context') with explicit language allowing reads for context and prohibiting writes/edits/build until plan is approved. Update README to match v1.1.0 questions-in-chat flow and align install-config version with snippet header.
Questions move to chat before the plan file is written. Plan file is the confirmed spec, not the place where open questions live.
Initial release. Plan file + questions before any skill/routine/app/instruction build. Split from self-iteration-loop use case.

post-install.name: Introduce the new capability
post-install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.
